result.report:
All audit numbers cross-check. One new decisive measurement from the DP above: order-2 pinned diagonal spaces are **1.6×10²⁷–7.8×10²⁸ per class** (exact counts, classes 0/100/312/624) versus 3.03×10⁷ for order 3 — so the order-3 diagonal-leaf cube architecture categorically does not transfer to order 2. The plan below is built around that fact.

---

# Engineering plan: closing ORDER 3 (4 classes) and ORDER 2 (625 classes)

## The structural fact the plan is built on

Every audited proposal leaves the diag × row-1 enumeration wall intact (~8.3×10²⁰ prefixes/skeleton for order 3 after symmetry; ≥10⁷ core-years at 28M nodes/s). The certified algebraic reductions (quotient 8x, strip+Gram 10²–10³, MDD 2–6x) compose to ~10³·⁵–10⁴·⁵ on the DFS lane — leaving a 10²·⁵–10⁴ core-year deficit. **Pure enumeration cannot close even one skeleton.** The only audited mechanism that does not pay the wall by enumeration is incremental CDCL clause learning across the canonical cube family: learned cross-row conflicts prune all later cubes instead of being rediscovered per leaf. So the decision lane is SAT-based; the algebra is deployed as model shrinkers and propagators inside it, and the DFS survives only as a cross-check and cube-survivor dumper.

## 1. Ranked stack (all components compose; factors are audited, not proposed)

| # | Component | Audited factor / role | Cost |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Certified full-G canonical diagonal cubes + complement pairing** (orderly-generation and diag-first lenses are the same quotient — both audits independently measured the identical 10,113,829-cube transversal: 5,056,913 + 2×2,528,458, 3 instances). Plus the audit's correction: the current break is factor 3 not 6, and σ₋₁ stabilizes every order-3 leaf — wire it as a free in-cube break. | 4.0x cubes, ~8x DFS work (exact, measured); order 2: 625→314 instances (ships to CP-SAT lane today, saves ~6,220 core-h/sweep) | 9-line dump patch + ~190 LOC canonicalizer (already demonstrated); VeriPB lex-leader + cover certificate 1–2 wks |
| 2 | **Strip-and-Schur model reduction + corner-forcing propagator** (absorbs the sound residue of the lattice-enumeration and dual-character lenses). 540→360 bits (order 3), 810→540 (order 2); 15 corner blocks + the 130 latest-closing equations become equality forcings at 12–25 µs (int64-safe). MANDATORY: per-embedding signs (PSD on QR, NSD on QNR embeddings — the uniform-PSD version is a false-UNSAT generator), lex-first-basis split over ≤126 pivot sets, skeleton-(w,z) decode, full 585/855-equation re-verification at leaves. Keep the interlacing propagator alongside (audited: not subsumed). | ~10²⁸·⁶–10²⁹·⁴ branch space removed per cube; the strongest single change to the SAT lane (180/270 fewer binary vars + equality propagation) | 1 wk leaf-filter graft; 3–6 wks full IPASIR-UP propagator; Lean lemma 1–2 wks (cheap, high audit value) |
| 3 | **Master incremental cube-and-conquer**: 3 master instances (order 3), assumption cubes over the 10.1M transversal, clause DB retained per shard, with the IPASIR-UP stack: per-embedding interlacing (a=4 QR / a=5 QNR), corner Schur forcing, Gram-linear row-2/3 derivations (low-rank lens, audited 10²–10³ at the frontier where node mass actually sits), repaired K–M variety eigenvalue-count cuts (per-class patterns + parity lemma; ≤1–2 orders, measure on frontier first), order-2 XOR layer (entailed; CDCL-only value). | The decision mechanism. Unknown factor — pilot (Experiment 1) measures it. | driver 200–400 LOC; pilot 1 day |
| 4 | **Solver engineering**: SBVA, order/totalizer encodings, 3-config kill-on-first portfolio (budget at ~1x CPU, race only on wall-clock), exact MDD relaxation counts as the cube-balancing signal (leaf counts vary <2.5%, so this is the only signal), static R-feature ordering (free — the SP "signal" reduced to exactly this). | 10–100x generic, literature-calibrated | ~1 wk |
| 5 | **In-cube strict Z3 break (g=16) + freeness theorem + count audits** at corrected moduli (mod-6 global order 3 / mod-9 order 2, mod-3 per fiber). Survives cube boundaries, which generic breaking does not. | 1.5–3x + retraction-class bug detection | 2–4 days |
| 6 | **GF(2)+cardinality certifier lottery** on all 629 classes, 1h cap; any UNSAT is a *lead* requiring certified replay (VeriPB/GJ-off rerun), never a closure by itself. | <0.3 firing probability priced; per-class closure 10–100x if it fires | ~$30–50, half a day |
| 7 | Conditional: **row-MDD membership propagator** in the DFS lane only if the head-to-head clears its pre-registered 3x gate (audited prediction 2–6x — at the line). | 2–6x on the cross-check lane only | 1–2 wks, gated |

**Combined estimate, ORDER 3 (3 effective instances, 10.1M cubes):** everything reduces to the warm per-cube conquer cost C.
- C ≤ 0.1 s → ~280 core-h (≈ $15–30 at spot)
- C ≈ 1 s → ~2,800 core-h (≈ $150–300)
- C ≈ 10 s → ~28,000 core-h (≈ $1.5–3k)
- C ≥ minutes (cubes behave like mini-CP-SAT-UNKNOWNs) → **no current path**; the stack fails and a new row-level theorem is required.

VeriPB logging adds 2–5x runtime; the pairing/twist lemmas live outside VeriPB as separately machine-checked isomorphisms (audited as required packaging).

**Combined estimate, ORDER 2 (314 instances):** my DP measurement (above) kills diagonal-leaf cubing: 10²⁷–10²⁸·⁹ pinned diagonals per class. Architecture instead: strip-reduced 540-bit master instances, cube on the first 2–3 diagonal blocks (C(18,r) ≈ 8.5k–48.6k per block → 10⁴–10⁹ cubes/instance, tunable), conquer phase carries essentially the whole load, XOR layer + GJ active. If order-3 per-cube economics transfer at this coarser granularity: 314 × (10⁵–10⁷ cubes) × ~1 s ≈ 10⁴–10⁵·⁵ core-h ≈ $500–$30k. That transfer is the speculative step — coarser cubes mean harder conquer, and the DFS engine cannot even build candidate tables at 18 cosets, so there is no enumeration fallback at all for order 2.

## 2. Three cheapest decisive experiments (in order)

1. **Incremental C&C pilot, hardened per audit** (<1 day, <$10): class 0, master CNF in both plain and strip-reduced form; run ≥10⁵ canonical cubes (not 10⁴ — clause-DB bloat appears late) through incremental CaDiCaL in the real 64-way sharded topology, P(9)/P(25) SAT anchors first. Record warm median/p90 per-cube time, fraction killed by propagation alone (primary go/no-go signal), conflict trend. Go: warm ≤1 s and falling. Kill: >10 s flat. This single number decides whether any budget closes anything.
2. **Strip/corner + Gram-derivation agreement test on real correlated survivors** (<1 day): instrument cyclo_dfs to dump (diag, rows-0-1) survivors and complete strips from one leaf of skeleton 0; run exact corner forcing and row-2/3 linear derivations; require exact agreement with all DFS extensions (any mismatch = bug or unsound step); measure realized per-corner rejection (kill <10x) and row-3 survivors per row-2 survivor (settles the independence-vs-Rényi-2 regime — decides whether the rows-5-9 completion engine has any realized weight). Pre-gate: Schur decode on P(9)/P(25)/Petersen, plus the QNR-sign regression (a planted {θ⁵,τ⁴} spectrum must NOT be pruned).
3. **Certifier lottery + GJ head-to-head + order-2 granularity table** (<1 day, <$50): (a) CMS/Bosphorus sweep of all 629 emitted CNF-XOR instances at 1h cap on Modal, pre-registered expectation INDETERMINATE, any UNSAT escalated to certified replay; (b) GJ-on vs GJ-off conflict counts on one pinned order-2 skeleton (prices the XOR lane honestly); (c) extend today's DP to all 625 classes (~25 min) and tabulate first-k-block cube counts to fix order-2 cube granularity before any order-2 engineering.

## 3. Refuted or audited down — do not re-propose

- **Rank-4 bordering "PSD at every embedding"**: false — NSD at QNR embeddings (the multiplicity trap); standalone strip Fincke–Pohst dead (corner pass rate 10⁻²·⁷; headline 10⁸–10¹¹ → component status only).
- **Dual-character standalone row-major DFS**: Knuth probe 6×10¹⁵–4×10¹⁹ nodes/leaf vs 10² required — mid-row boxes prune nothing; demoted to model-reduction ingredients.
- **K–M m-level pipeline**: aconv equations do not descend to m (measured 5.7×10¹⁶ m-rows/row vs claimed 4×10⁹); as written, uniform caps false-UNSAT the QNR classes; salvage = variety-theorem cuts at fine level only.
- **Survey propagation including its salvage**: marginals 100% R-determined (proven-blind class), cross-seed ρ=0.038; zero further effort; read block ordering off R for free.
- **SDP "completion-collapse lemma" as filed**: two-sided DKW clause refuted by explicit counterexample; file only the restricted (chordal/GJSW + diagonal-frontier) version; convex per-node gates closed (0-fire, ceiling 1.28x); the k≥6 minor-multiplicity carve-out is realized by strip rank-4 forcing.
- **XOR-layer structural claims**: layer is entailed — 0/2312 skeleton kills measured, pinned parities are exactly the 2 feasible vectors (cube prune = 1), the 10¹⁵→10¹⁰ row claim was a baseline swap; survives as CDCL propagation aid only.
- **H9-shadow cube layer**: measured a_c = 0.995 vs ≤0.44 required (its own kill criterion), F ≈ 10⁵⁹ cubes; and the H4/Z2 in-cube break for order 2 is UNSOUND (QNR decimation flips a — the retraction-class gauge trap).
- **Full 9-row MDD join**: dead (×304/row frontier growth); per-cube MB certificates are an exabyte-scale non-starter; row-MDD propagator audited to 2–6x, at its own kill line.
- **σ-orbit equation dropping**: unsound (Z13 counterexample).
- **S3×S3 / incidence-pattern diagonal quotient (3,765 cubes)**: unsound; AlgAut = C12 exactly; symmetry ceiling |G| ≤ 24 is proven — no orders of magnitude from symmetry, ever.
- **"2⁻³⁶ expected refutable" certifier argument**: unsound (Macaulay rows ≠ independent parities; emitted XOR systems are linearly consistent); lottery priced at <0.3.
- **Low-rank 10⁷²/10¹⁰⁸ headlines**: vacuous raw-space figures; realized lane factor 10²–10³; ~1x decision-level standalone.
- **"Current decimation break = 6" (order 3)**: measured 3 (σ₋₁ fixes every diagonal) — a correction that *gives* a free extra 2x in-cube.
- **Audit moduli mod-12/mod-18**: corrected to mod-6 / mod-9 on pinned counts.

## 4. Honest bottom line

- **$1k**: ORDER 3 closes *if and only if* Experiment 1 passes at warm ≤1–10 s/cube (run cost $15–$3k including certification overhead). I put ~40–60% on the pilot passing — incremental C&C has the right mechanism for exactly this failure mode (cross-row conflicts rediscovered per leaf), but 10.1M assumption calls is 1–2 orders beyond the literature regime. 8/8: no.
- **$10k**: ORDER 3 closed with full VeriPB certification and retries even in the 10 s/cube branch, plus the order-2 openers (314-instance strip-reduced CP-SAT sweep, certifier lottery, high-Aut classes). 8/8: unlikely (~10–15%).
- **$100k**: 8/8 plausible but not likely (~25%). Order 2's diagonal space is 10²⁷–10²⁹ per class (measured today), so its conquer phase must do what enumeration never can; the realistic success path is the order-3 run revealing that most cubes die by propagation against the learned clause DB (clause mass saturates), in which case coarse-cube order 2 inherits that saturation and lands in the $10k–$50k band. If it does not saturate, no budget closes order 2 — the gap is a theorem, not compute.
- **Single biggest remaining uncertainty**: whether the cross-row UNSAT conflict mass is *finitely learnable* — operationally, the warm per-cube refutation cost in incremental CDCL over the canonical cube family. It is one number, it is measured by Experiment 1 for under $10 in under a day, and every other element of the stack is a certified constant factor that cannot substitute for it.
